object_types.description: Per-subphase LD (Liquidated Damages) exposure analysis combining deterministic, Monte Carlo, and window-aware Monte Carlo models. Each row represents a construction subphase and its total LD risk profile across all three modeling tiers.

mcLdStd.description: Monte Carlo standard deviation of LD exposure

winProbAnyDelay.description: Window-aware MC probability of any delay

mcLdP5.description: Monte Carlo 5th percentile LD — best-case scenario

mcDelayMean.description: Monte Carlo average delay in days

pad.description: Contractual Power Available Date for this subphase

dailyLdRate.description: Contractual daily LD rate in USD

mcModalBottleneckPct.description: Percentage of MC iterations where the modal bottleneck asset was the bottleneck

mcProbAnyDelay.description: Monte Carlo probability that at least one asset causes delay past the PAD

bottleneckAsset.description: Asset with the latest ready date (deterministic bottleneck)

mcModalBottleneckAsset.description: Asset most frequently the bottleneck across MC iterations

mcLdMean.description: Monte Carlo expected (mean) LD exposure in USD

assetsNearRisk.description: Count of assets with buffer between 0 and 14 days

mcLdP90.description: Monte Carlo 90th percentile LD exposure — typical downside scenario

detLdExposure.description: Deterministic LD exposure in USD based on single-point bottleneck delay

assetsLate.description: Count of assets with negative buffer (past PAD)

mcLdP25.description: Monte Carlo 25th percentile LD — lower quartile

riskCategory.description: Compound risk classification: COMPOUND, SINGLE_BOTTLENECK, MULTIPLE_AT_RISK, WATCH, or OK

lateAssets.description: Comma-separated list of asset numbers currently late

winLdMean.description: Window-aware MC expected LD exposure using P6 delivery windows

detDelayDays.description: Deterministic delay in calendar days beyond the Power Available Date

object_types.description: Per-asset LD exposure analysis combining deterministic risk assessment, Monte Carlo bottleneck frequency, root cause diagnosis, and suggested mitigation actions. Provides a unified risk view for each asset across all three modeling tiers.

subphaseId.description: Foreign key to CPS Subphase and LD Subphase Analysis

trueBufferDays.description: Calendar days between asset ready date and PAD. Negative = late.

suggestedAction.description: Recommended mitigation action for the at-risk asset

readyDate.description: Estimated date asset will be ready (delivery + post-delivery work)

ldExposureIfBottleneck.description: LD exposure amount if this asset becomes the subphase bottleneck

mcBottleneckFrequency.description: Fraction of MC iterations where this asset was the subphase bottleneck

rootCause.description: Diagnosed root cause category for the asset being at risk

winExpectedLdContribution.description: Expected LD contribution from window-aware MC

mcAtRiskFrequency.description: Fraction of MC iterations where this asset was at risk

severity.description: Bottleneck diagnosis severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, or WATCH

mcExpectedLdContribution.description: Expected LD contribution from this asset across MC iterations

p6Ead.description: P6-derived earliest access date for delivery

diagnosis.description: Detailed diagnosis text explaining the asset's risk situation

postDeliveryDays.description: Estimated post-delivery work days before asset is ready

winBottleneckFrequency.description: Fraction of window-MC iterations where this asset was the bottleneck

ldRiskFlag.description: Asset LD risk classification: LATE, AT_RISK, TIGHT, or OK

p6DeliveryFloatHrs.description: P6 float in hours between EAD and delivery date

object_types.description: Represents the intent to send a notification as part of the Reactive MX workflow. Supports conditional logic, scheduling, escalation timers, and audit trails. Gets processed to create actual SMS Messages or other notification types.

escalationRequestId.description: ID of the notification request to create if escalation deadline passes

notificationRequestId.description: Unique identifier for the notification request

recipientTechnicianId.description: ID of the technician to notify (if recipient is a technician)

errorMessage.description: Error details if the notification request failed

notificationType.description: Type of notification (incident_reported, tech_assigned, tech_acknowledged, escalation, tech_arrived, status_update, work_completed)

priority.description: Priority level for the notification (Low, Normal, High, Urgent)

createdAt.description: When the notification request was created

recipientCoordinatorId.description: ID of the coordinator to notify (if recipient is a coordinator)

recipientType.description: Type of recipient (coordinator, technician, ops_lead, parts_manager)

createdBy.description: User or system that created the notification request

processedAt.description: When the notification request was processed

resultingMessageId.description: ID of the SMS Message created from this notification request

workOrderId.description: ID of the work order that triggered this notification

triggerSource.description: What triggered this notification request (e.g., Incident status change, Work order assignment)

scheduledAt.description: When to send the notification (null means send immediately)

requestStatus.description: Current status of the notification request (Pending, Scheduled, Processing, Sent, Cancelled, Failed)

action_types.toolDescription: Runs a Monte Carlo availability simulation on a reliability system. Use this when the user wants to simulate, analyze availability, or test what-if scenarios. Takes the system to simulate plus configuration: simulation_hours (default 8760 = 1 year), num_iterations (default 500, max 100000), and time_step_hours (default 1.0). Returns results including mean availability, standard deviation, confidence intervals, and mean downtime hours. Results are saved as a SimulationRun object and also written back to the system's lastMeanAvailability, lastMeanDowntimeHours, and lastSimulationDate properties.

query_types.description:
Computes fleet-level urgency metrics for display in a Workshop table.

Returns a struct per fleet with:
  - unscheduledPm / unscheduledReactive: WOs with dueHours ≤ 250, no assigned tech, not Closed
  - stalePm / staleReactive: WOs scheduled in the past (endTime < now), not Completed/Closed
  - overduePmAssets: unique assets with at least one overdue task (dueHours < 0)
  - overduePmTasks: total count of overdue tasks

All WO/task queries are done in bulk across all fleets, then mapped back.

(dueHours < 0, not Completed or Closed).

Returns a labelled string per fleet, e.g. "Total Overdue PM Tasks: 35".

Run all tests in an evaluation config (A/B testing).

    Args:
        config_id: Evaluation config primary key
        num_trials: Number of trials per test (1-10)

    Returns:
        Empty list (results saved to ontology)

    Example:
        runConfigAction(
            config_id="config-abc-123",
            num_trials=3
        )
Get username from user ID.

Reject a knowledge proposal.

    Args:
        proposal_pk: Proposal ID to reject
        rejection_reason: Why rejecting

    Returns:
        JSON with result

Execute code in context of a specific log (for debugging).

    This function recreates the execution environment from a specific log,
    including artifacts and custom ontology SDKs if the agent has them.

    Args:
        log_pk: UUID of the log to recreate environment from
        execution_code: Python code to execute in that environment

    Returns:
        Markdown-formatted execution results

Force refresh the cached SDK for an A4dOntologyConfiguration.

    This function:
    1. Removes the cached SDK from the local filesystem
    2. Forces a fresh download from the configuration's latest_osdk attachment
    3. Reinstalls the SDK to the cache directory

    Use this when:
    - The SDK has been regenerated and you want agents to use the new version immediately
    - The cached SDK is corrupted
    - You want to ensure agents are using the absolute latest SDK

    Args:
        configuration_primary_key: Primary key of the A4dOntologyConfiguration

    Returns:
        list[OntologyEdit]: Empty list (no ontology edits made)

    Example:
        # After regenerating SDK, force agents to use the new version
        force_refresh_ontology_configuration_sdk_cache("healthcare_omop_config")

For each fleet in the input set, computes four metrics displayed as a
text-based summary table:

1. Unscheduled WO's (<250 hrs): WOs with dueHours ≤ 250, no assigned
   technician, not Closed — split by PM vs Reactive maintenance type.
2. Stale WO's: WOs scheduled in the past (endTime < now) that are not
   Completed or Closed — split by PM vs Reactive.
3. Assets with Overdue PM Tasks: count of unique assets having at least
   one task where dueHours < 0, not Completed/Closed.
4. Total Count of Overdue PM Tasks: total count of such overdue tasks.

Returns a formatted multi-line string per fleet, e.g.:

  Unsched WO's (<250h) | PM: 0 | Reactive: 3
  Stale WO's | PM: 2 | Reactive: 5
  Assets w/ Overdue PM Tasks: 12
  Total Overdue PM Tasks: 35

Get version history for a knowledge node.

    Args:

    Returns:
        JSON with all versions

    Example:
        get_knowledge_node_history("abc-123-def-456")

query_types.description:
Create a new version of an existing artifact with manually edited content.

    This function takes an artifact (identified by its version-specific artifact_pk),
    creates a new version with the provided content, and persists it to Foundry.
    The original generation code is preserved with a "THIS CODE IS OUTDATED"
    annotation to indicate the artifact was manually edited.

    Supported artifact types: json, markdown, html, text, str.

    Args:
        artifact_pk: The artifact_pk (primary key) of the specific version to edit.
                     This is the version-specific PK, NOT the stable artifact_identifier.
        new_content: The new content as a string. For json artifacts, must be valid JSON.

    Returns:
        Markdown-formatted summary of the edit result.
A light wrapper around the Rebalance Work Order function - making this since we cannot do a search around directly in Automate to trigger a rebalance from task time update.
Get current user's name.

Finds ranked swap candidates at a destination milestone for a given asset.
Returns a JSON string containing the reallocation analysis.

Copy an analysis and all its internals (logs, artifacts, infra logs).

    Supports branching: pass a log PK to copy only the log chain up to that
    point, together with the artifacts and infra logs that belong to it.

    Args:
        source_analysis_id: PK of the analysis to copy.
        new_name: Name for the copy. Defaults to "Copy of <name or pk>".
        new_description: Description for the copy. Defaults to
            "Copy of <original description>".
        up_to_log_pk: Log PK to branch at. When set, only the ancestor
            chain of this log (inclusive) is copied.

    Returns:
        JSON with the new analysis ID and copy statistics.

For each fleet, counts the number of unique assets that have at least one
overdue task (dueHours < 0, not Completed or Closed).

Returns a labelled string per fleet, e.g. "Assets w/ Overdue PM Tasks: 12".

For each fleet, counts work orders that are "stale":
  - scheduled in the past (endTime < now)
  - not Completed and not Closed

Returns a string per fleet formatted as "PM: X | Reactive: Y".

Run a unit test against an agent.

    Args:
        unit_test_pk: Primary key of the unit test
        agent_id: Agent to test
        num_trials: Number of times to run (1-10) for consistency checking

    Returns:
        Empty list (results saved to ontology)

    Example:
        runUnitTestAction(
            unit_test_pk="test-abc-123",
            agent_id="agent-xyz-456",
            num_trials=5
        )

Finds ALL reallocation options for an asset in a single server call.

1. Resolves the asset's current requirement and origin milestone.
2. Discovers all other milestones that have requirements for the same itemId.
3. Evaluates each destination milestone in parallel.
4. Returns a JSON string with { eligible: boolean, results: ReallocationResult[] }.

Create or update a knowledge node manually (by human user).

    For creation: Leave knowledge_identifier empty
    For update: Provide existing knowledge_identifier

    Args:
        validation_status: Validation status (e.g., "APPROVED", "PENDING_REVIEW")
        title: Short description of the knowledge node
        value: The actual content/knowledge
        tags: Comma-separated tags for categorization
        knowledge_identifier: Leave empty for new node, or provide existing identifier to create new version

    Returns:
        JSON with created/updated knowledge node details
Return both the OSDK library version and the backend release version.
Get the most recent log PK for an analysis.

Returns a string per fleet formatted as "PM: X | Reactive: Y".

query_types.description:
Generate SDK and documentation for an A4dOntologyConfiguration.

    This function:
    1. Fetches the A4dOntologyConfiguration object
    2. Generates SDK using foundry_sdk_generator with the configured APIs
    3. Packages the SDK as a .tar.gz file
    4. Uploads it to the configuration's latest_osdk attachment property
    5. Generates documentation for all object types and stores in created_docs

    Args:
        configuration_primary_key: Primary key of the A4dOntologyConfiguration

    Returns:
        str: Status message describing what happened (success or error details)

    Example:
        refresh_ontology_configuration_sdk_and_docs(
            configuration_primary_key="healthcare_omop_config"
        )
Upload a file as an artifact to an analysis.
